prompt:
Weekly freight review needs numbers, not a spreadsheet ritual. Build `haulstats.ps1` in the repo root: `pwsh -NoProfile -File haulstats.ps1 -Path shipments.csv`, where the CSV has at least Region, Carrier, WeightKg columns. It prints a rollup CSV to stdout, one row per (Region, Carrier) pair.

House style, non-negotiable: aggregation goes through the object pipeline — Group-Object for the buckets, Measure-Object for the numbers. The harness greps the source for both, because the last hand-rolled version of this had a running-total bug for a month.

Details that matter:

- Output columns, exactly: Region, Carrier, Shipments, TotalKg, MinKg, MaxKg, AvgKg. Emit through ConvertTo-Csv with its defaults — the harness compares stdout byte-for-byte, header included.
- The feed is dirty: region strings arrive in mixed case and 'APAC' vs 'apac' are DIFFERENT buckets until the upstream fix lands (Group-Object groups case-insensitively unless you tell it otherwise — tell it otherwise). Row order: ordinal by Region then Carrier, so 'APAC' sorts before 'apac' and 'Brightline' before 'apex'. Do not let a culture-aware sort interleave them.
- Weights: parse WeightKg as an invariant-culture double. A value that won't parse aborts the whole report: stderr `haulstats: bad WeightKg 'heavy' at data row 2` (1-based data row, header not counted), exit 65, nothing on stdout.
- Shipments is the group's row count. TotalKg/MinKg/MaxKg from Measure-Object. AvgKg = total/count rounded to 2 decimals, HALF AWAY FROM ZERO (finance asked; 4.375 prints 4.38). All four weight columns formatted with exactly two decimals, invariant culture.
- A header-only feed prints just the report header line, exit 0. A rowed feed missing a required column: stderr `haulstats: required column 'WeightKg' not found`, exit 65. Missing file: `haulstats: file not found: <path>`, exit 66.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_haulstats.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P3 item 3: Group-Object composite key + Measure-Object sum/min/max with calculated properties, ordinal group ordering, ConvertTo-Csv byte-exact. PowerShell-native pins: Group-Object -CaseSensitive on a two-property key (default grouping is case-insensitive — 'APAC'/'apac' merge without it, probed 2026-07-16), Measure-Object -Sum -Minimum -Maximum over typed doubles, property-bag report rows serialized by ConvertTo-Csv (7.6 default quotes every field — pinned). Ordinal ordering pitfalls this seed pins: default Sort-Object is culture-linguistic; also probed that [Array]::Sort(keys, items, comparer) with an object[] items array silently sorts a CONVERTED COPY (PowerShell binds the generic overload), so the reference sorts key strings and rebuilds via a case-SENSITIVE Dictionary (plain @{} hashtables are OrdinalIgnoreCase-keyed and would merge 'APAC'/'apac' too). AvgKg 4.375->4.38 pins MidpointRounding::AwayFromZero. Differentiation: bash-poolsheet/bash-dureport aggregate text with awk-style sums; here the contract is object-pipeline grouping metadata and typed measurement, plus case-sensitivity semantics text tools don't have. No overlap with ps-textgrid (fixed-width rendering) — output here is machine CSV.
